svg.text: Event-Driven FHIR Processing Internals
Technical view: producer-consumer topology, cache roles, reliability, and tenant-safe observability
Ingestion + Canonicalization
Producers: Customer FHIR server, customer CDR feeds, subscription triggers
Canonical event envelope from FHIR payloads
Tenant auth + boundary enforcement at gateway
Internal FHIR server as normalized source-of-truth
Kafka Topology
Topics: hdim.patient.events.v1, hdim.care_gap.events.v1, hdim.quality.events.v1, hdim.actions.events.v1
Consumer groups: hdim.patient-service.cg.v1, hdim.care-gap-service.cg.v1, hdim.quality-measure-service.cg.v1, hdim.action-service.cg.v1
Retry pipeline: hdim.patient.events.retry.v1 -> bounded attempts -> hdim.patient.events.dlq.v1
Partition strategy per tenant + patient key
Redis State Layer
Patient Snapshot Cache for low-latency context
Rule Context Cache for policy/guideline lookup
Idempotency Keys to prevent duplicate actions
TTL and PHI-safe key/value design
Deterministic Processing and Delivery
FHIR ingestion -> canonical event -> topic fan-out -> state-aware rule evaluation -> action publish
Write-back targets: Task, CarePlan, Communication with idempotent guardrails
Observability: consumer lag, trace propagation, audit stream, error-class dashboards
Security: tenant context propagation, PHI-safe logging boundaries, API auth controls
Canonical Journey (unchanged from other tabs)
1 Patient Event Created | 2 Data Ingested | 3 Kafka Stream Routed | 4 Service Processing | 5 Redis Context Lookup
6 Care Rule Evaluation | 7 Action Generated | 8 Consumer Delivery | 9 Outcome + Audit
Real-world reference framing: Healthix, HealthInfoNet, Verato
